# Code Anchors: Issue-Sourced Planning

- `libs/epic-resolve/src/resolve.ts` — orchestrates the reconstruction: fetch, build the dependency graph, serialize, and write the materialized epic, failing closed if any referenced sub-issue is unfetchable.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/gh.ts` — fetches the epic issue, its story sub-issues, and their native dependency edges from GitHub (read-only).
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/serialize.ts` — canonical, byte-stable serialization of the epic field shape, so an unchanged issue graph yields byte-identical output.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/meta.ts` — reads the hidden frontmatter machine comment off the issue body, falling back to recoverable fields alone for a hand-filed epic.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/render.ts` — renders the resolved epic into the existing epic markdown shape downstream parsers already read.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/write.ts` — writes the materialized epic to a path outside version control the working tree never reports as new.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/run.ts` — the resolver CLI entry point.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/diagnostic.ts` — the fail-closed diagnostics emitted on an unfetchable sub-issue or a non-epic target.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/serialize.spec.ts` — determinism/idempotency coverage: byte-identical output on an unchanged issue graph.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/resolve.spec.ts` — end-to-end reconstruction and fail-closed (no partial output) coverage.
- `libs/epic-resolve/src/meta.spec.ts` — frontmatter round-trip and recoverable-only fallback coverage.
- `.claude/skills/nxs-epic-resolve/scripts/epic_resolve.ts` — the skill CLI wrapping the resolver for the pipeline stages.
- `.claude/skills/nxs-epic-resolve/SKILL.md` — the resolver skill: the single producer of the materialized epic every stage reads.
- `.claude/skills/nxs-gh-create-epic/scripts/nxs_gh_create_epic.py` — embeds the raw planning frontmatter in a hidden machine comment on the epic issue so the resolver can round-trip it.
- `.claude/commands/nxs.epic.md` — files the epic and story issues at approval and commits nothing to the queue; supports the --from import that pulls a hand-filed epic through the resolver.
- `.claude/commands/nxs.hld.md` — obtains the epic through the resolver instead of a committed queue file.
- `.claude/commands/nxs.analyze.md` — obtains the epic's stories, acceptance criteria, and success metrics through the resolver.
- `.claude/commands/nxs.close.md` — materializes the epic via the resolver and writes the born-at-close queue entry.
- `.gitignore` — excludes the materialized epic's output location from version control.
